response.justification: Candidate is dramatically overqualified for a Junior/entry-level role, with 15+ years of experience at Staff/Senior level. Java appears only as a peripheral skill in a stack dominated by TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang, with no demonstrated insurance-sector or recent Java project work. German proficiency is listed as 'Basic', falling well short of the strong written and verbal German required for client-facing work in Germany.
strengths: CS degree and deep software craftsmanship background (TDD, CI/CD, agile)
Based in Berlin, comfortable with travel and client collaboration
Broad polyglot exposure including some Java and strong OOP fundamentals
concerns: Massively overqualified — Staff/Senior engineer applying to Junior role suggests poor fit, retention risk, and salary mismatch
German is only 'Basic', but role requires strong German for insurance clients
Java is a minor part of the stack; no recent or substantial Java project experience

key_factors.factor: Strong engineering fundamentals and agile experience
